'''Kristian John Radlinski''' [[Member of the Most Excellent Order of the British Empire|MBE]] (born 9 April 1976) is an English [[rugby league]] executive who is [[Chief Executive Officer|CEO]] of the [[Wigan Warriors]] in the [[Super League]] and a former professional rugby league footballer. 

He played his entire professional career for Wigan, making over 300 appearances between 1993 and 2006, playing mainly as a {{rlp|fb}}. He also won 10 caps for [[England national rugby league team|England]] and 20 caps for [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] at international level.

==Background==
Radlinski was born in [[Wigan]], [[Greater Manchester]], England. His family originated in Poland and have lived in Wigan since World War II.

==Playing career==
===Club career===
Radlinski played for [[Wigan St Judes]] and [[Wigan St Patricks]] at amateur level. He signed for Wigan, his hometown club, in 1993, aged 17, and went on to play his entire professional career for them. He debuted for the first team as a substitute in October 1993 against [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]].<ref name="Radlinski inducted into Hall of Fame">{{cite web |title=Radlinski inducted into Hall of Fame |url=https://wiganwarriors.com/blog/2022/09/04/radlinski-inducted-into-hall-of-fame/ |website=Wigan Warriors |access-date=22 January 2024 |date=4 September 2022}}</ref> 

He made his first start in November 1994, scoring a try in a 30–6 win against [[Featherstone Rovers]]. He spent most of the [[1994–95 Rugby Football League season|1994–95 season]] playing on the [[wing (rugby league)|wing]], deputising for [[Jason Robinson (rugby)|Jason Robinson]] or [[Martin Offiah]] if they were injured.<ref>{{cite news |last1=Irvine |first1=Christopher |title=Offiah turns down more money to stay with Wigan |url=https://archive.org/details/NewsUK1995UKEnglish/Apr%2026%201995%2C%20The%20Times%2C%20%2365251%2C%20UK%20%28en%29/page/n37/mode/2up |work=The Times |date=26 April 1995 |page=38}}</ref> In May 1995, at age 19, he scored a hat-trick in the [[1994–95 Rugby League Premiership|1995 Premiership]] final against [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]], becoming the first player to do so in a [[Rugby League Premiership|Premiership]] final, and was also the youngest ever winner of the [[Harry Sunderland Trophy]] for man-of-the-match in Wigan's victory.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/radlinski-rampant-in-wigan-rout-1620684.html|title = Radlinski rampant in Wigan rout|website = [[Independent.co.uk]]|date = 21 May 1995}}</ref>

Radlinski played at {{rlp|ce}} and scored a try in Wigan's 25–16 victory over [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] in the [[1995–96 Regal Trophy]] Final during the [[1995–96 Rugby Football League season|1995–96 season]] at [[John Smith's Stadium|Alfred McAlpine Stadium]], [[Huddersfield]] on Saturday 13 January 1996.<ref name="13th January 1996: St Helens 16 Wigan 25 (Regal Trophy Final)">{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=2976|title=13th January 1996: St Helens 16 Wigan 25 (Regal Trophy Final)|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2014|access-date=1 January 2015|archive-date=7 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181007005311/http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=2976|url-status=dead}}</ref>

Radlinski was a [[Rugby League Premiership|Premiership]] winner with Wigan, playing {{rlp|fb}} in their 44–14 victory over [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] in the Rugby League Premiership Final during [[Super League I]] at [[Old Trafford]], [[Manchester]] on Sunday 8 September 1996.<ref name="8th September 1996: St Helens 14 Wigan 44 (Premiership Trophy Final)">{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=3071|title=8th September 1996: St Helens 14 Wigan 44 (Premiership Trophy Final)|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2014|access-date=1 January 2015}}</ref>

Radlinski was named in the [[Super League Dream Team]] of 1998. He also appeared at fullback in Wigan's [[1998 Super League Grand Final]] victory over [[Leeds Rhinos]]. Radlinski played for Wigan at centre in their [[2000 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by St Helens.

Radlinski played at {{rlp|fb}} in Wigan's [[2001 Super League Grand Final]] loss against the [[Bradford Bulls]].
In the 2002 [[Challenge Cup]] Final at [[Murrayfield Stadium]] Radlinski won the [[Lance Todd Trophy]] for man of the match. Kris competed in this match despite being in hospital all week prior to Wigan's 21–12 victory over St Helens with a serious foot infection.

Radlinski played for Wigan at {{rlp|fb}} in the [[2003 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by Bradford Bulls.

He was given a testimonial year in 2005 for his loyal service to Wigan Warriors. Radlinski made only 15 appearances for the team during a season that was interrupted by multiple injuries.<ref>{{cite web |title=Radlinski blow for luckless Wigan |url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/super_league/wigan/4614783.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=23 January 2024 |date=6 June 2005}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Injury ends season for Radlinski |url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/super_league/wigan/4697015.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=23 January 2024 |date=3 August 2005}}</ref> His testimonial match was played at the [[JJB Stadium]] on 2 February 2006 against the [[Huddersfield Giants]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Wigan confirm Radlinski benefit |url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/super_league/wigan/4472370.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=23 January 2024 |date=25 November 2005}}</ref> Wigan won this match 38–22.<ref>{{cite web |title=Sharp given Super-style headache |url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/news/sharp-given-super-style-headache-5061346 |website=YorkshireLive |access-date=23 January 2024 |date=3 February 2006}}</ref>

Radlinski announced his retirement from the sport prematurely on 2 March 2006 due to persistent injuries.<ref>{{cite web |title=Injury forces Radlinski to retire |url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/super_league/wigan/4765764.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=21 January 2024 |date=2 March 2006}}</ref>  He had made over 300 appearances for Wigan, scoring 183 tries.<ref>{{cite web |title=Radlinski - a true rugby league great |url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/super_league/wigan/4766372.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=23 January 2024 |date=2 March 2006}}</ref> This brought down the curtain on a successful career during which the player was a loyal servant to his club.

In June 2006 Radlinski came out of retirement and returned to Wigan for the remainder of 2006's [[Super League XI]], playing for no payment to aid the team during their personnel struggles that year.<ref>{{cite web |title=Radlinski comes out of retirement |url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/super_league/wigan/5071296.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=23 January 2024 |date=12 June 2006}}</ref> He helped the club avoid relegation, making six appearances before retiring a second time in August 2006.<ref>{{cite web |title=Radlinski calls time on comeback |url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/super_league/wigan/5274636.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=23 January 2024 |date=22 August 2006}}</ref>

===International career===
Radlinski was selected to play for [[England national rugby league team|England]] in the [[1995 Rugby League World Cup|1995 World Cup]] Final at {{rlp|fb}} in their defeat by [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]].

He won his first cap for [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] on the [[1996 Great Britain Lions tour|1996 Lions tour]]. In the 1997 post season, Radlinski was selected to play for Great Britain at centre in all three matches of the [[Super League Test series]] against [[Super League (Australia)#International_matches|Australia]].

Internationally he was capped 20 times by Great Britain, and won 10 England caps.

==Coaching and management==

Durin the 2009 season, Radlinski returned to Wigan, taking up a scholarship coaching role. He was part of the England coaching staff for the [[2009 Four Nations]] tournament. On 1 December 2009, Radlinski was appointed Rugby General Manager at Wigan, working alongside new head coach [[Michael Maguire (rugby league)|Michael Maguire]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://wwos.ninemsn.com.au/article.aspx?id=978970|title=Radlinski appointed Wigan manager|work=wwos|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20091206002228/http://wwos.ninemsn.com.au/article.aspx?id=978970|archive-date=6 December 2009|df=dmy-all}}</ref> 

In 2014, Radlinski was appointed an Executive Director of Wigan Warriors before being promoted to CEO on New Years Day 2023.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://wiganwarriors.com/blog/2023/01/27/radlinski-becomes-ceo/ | title=Radlinski becomes CEO | date=27 January 2023 }}</ref> Radlinski was a key figure in securing Wigan's place at the 2025 [[Rugby League Las Vegas]] event.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/inside-the-deal-how-wigan-warriors-secured-super-leagues-historic-las-vegas-trip | title=The inside story on how Wigan Warriors secured historic Las Vegas deal | date=9 July 2024 }}</ref><ref>{{cite news | url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/article/2024/jul/09/super-league-in-vegas-hailed-the-biggest-moment-in-its-28-year-history-rugby-league | title=Super League in Las Vegas hailed as 'biggest moment' in its 28-year history | newspaper=The Guardian | date=9 July 2024 | last1=Bower | first1=Aaron }}</ref>

==Other work==

Radlinski published his autobiography entitled ''Simply Rad'' in 2009.<ref>{{cite book 
|title= Simply Rad
|last= Radlinski
|first= Kris
|year= 2009
|publisher= Vertical Editions
|location= UK
|isbn= 9781904091349
|url= https://books.google.com/books?id=ZnFMPgAACAAJ
|access-date= 19 February 2011}}</ref>
